methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Float Cargo is a seed-stage transportation company founded by Conor Lenahan. The broader transportation sector contains seven private comparables and no public peers suitable for valuation triangulation. Top private peers identified include Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft, though these span significantly different stages and scales.

Peer-median methodology is not applicable to this analysis. While five same-sector peers exist in Transportation, none satisfy minimum cohort requirements—zero same-stage matches, one adjacent-stage match, and zero similar-tier matches were found. A threshold of at least three comparable datapoints is necessary for reliable peer-median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of transportation subsectors and development stages represented in the peer set.

Without sufficient comparable transaction or public market data, conventional triangulation methods cannot be applied with confidence. The valuation of Float Cargo at this stage should be informed by alternative methods including discounted cash flow projections (if applicable to seed-stage operations), founder track record assessment, and specific value-creation milestones. Key uncertainties include the company's current revenue, burn rate, and the specific transportation subsector and go-to-market approach—factors that would materially affect comparability to the identified peer universe. A traditional venture-backed valuation framework grounded in product-market fit evidence and capital requirements would be more appropriate than comps-based analysis at this juncture.
